subjects.name: Biology
subjects.description: Chapter-wise biology MCQ drills focused on core MDCAT concepts, human physiology, and quick recall.

subjects.name: Chemistry
subjects.description: High-yield chemistry practice covering conceptual theory, equations, bonding trends, and applied reasoning.

subjects.name: Physics
subjects.description: Numerical and concept-driven physics MCQs with formula fluency, motion, waves, and electrostatics.

subjects.name: English
subjects.description: MDCAT English practice for grammar, vocabulary, sentence correction, and reading-based questions.

chapters.name: Biological Molecules
chapters.description: Carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids.

chapters.name: Enzymes
chapters.description: Catalysis, factors affecting enzyme activity, and regulation.

chapters.name: Atomic Structure
chapters.description: Subatomic particles, isotopes, and electronic configuration.

chapters.name: Chemical Bonding
chapters.description: Ionic, covalent, coordinate, and molecular shapes.

chapters.name: Stoichiometry
chapters.description: Mole concept, chemical equations, and limiting reagent.

chapters.name: States of Matter
chapters.description: Gases, liquids, solids, and kinetic theory.

chapters.name: Organic Chemistry
chapters.description: Hydrocarbons, functional groups, and basic reactions.

mcqs.question: Which organelle is primarily responsible for ATP production in eukaryotic cells?
mcqs.explanation: Mitochondria generate most cellular ATP through aerobic respiration.

mcqs.question: The fluid mosaic model describes the structure of the:
mcqs.explanation: The fluid mosaic model explains the dynamic lipid bilayer and embedded proteins of the plasma membrane.

mcqs.question: Diffusion of water across a selectively permeable membrane is called:
mcqs.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of water from a region of higher water potential to lower water potential.

mcqs.question: Amino acids are the monomers of:
mcqs.explanation: Proteins are polymers made from amino acid monomers linked by peptide bonds.

mcqs.question: Which biomolecule stores genetic information?
mcqs.explanation: DNA and RNA are nucleic acids that store and transmit genetic information.

mcqs.question: The bond between two monosaccharides is called a:
mcqs.explanation: Monosaccharides join through glycosidic bonds to form disaccharides and polysaccharides.

mcqs.question: Enzymes speed up reactions by:
mcqs.explanation: Enzymes lower activation energy and increase the rate of reaction without being consumed.

mcqs.question: The region of an enzyme where the substrate binds is called the:
mcqs.explanation: The active site is complementary in shape and chemistry to the substrate.

mcqs.question: A sharp fall in enzyme activity at very high temperature usually occurs because of:
mcqs.explanation: Excess heat disrupts the enzyme's three-dimensional structure, causing denaturation.

mcqs.question: A trait that appears in the F1 generation of a monohybrid cross is generally:
mcqs.explanation: Dominant alleles express in the heterozygous F1 generation.

mcqs.question: DNA replication takes place during which phase of the cell cycle?
mcqs.explanation: DNA synthesis occurs during the S phase.

mcqs.question: The basic unit of heredity is the:
mcqs.explanation: A gene is a sequence of DNA that influences a specific characteristic.

mcqs.question: Which blood vessel carries oxygenated blood from lungs to heart?
mcqs.explanation: Pulmonary veins return oxygenated blood from the lungs to the left atrium.

mcqs.question: The functional unit of the kidney is the:
mcqs.explanation: The nephron filters blood and forms urine.

mcqs.question: Insulin mainly helps lower blood glucose by:
mcqs.explanation: Insulin promotes glucose uptake and storage, lowering blood glucose concentration.

mcqs.question: The atomic number of an element equals the number of:
mcqs.explanation: Atomic number is defined by the number of protons in the nucleus.

mcqs.question: Isotopes of an element differ in the number of:
mcqs.explanation: Isotopes have the same atomic number but different mass numbers due to different neutron counts.

mcqs.question: Which subatomic particle has a negative charge?
mcqs.explanation: Electrons carry a negative charge.

mcqs.question: A covalent bond is formed by:
mcqs.explanation: Atoms in covalent compounds share electron pairs.

mcqs.question: The bond in sodium chloride is predominantly:
mcqs.explanation: Sodium transfers an electron to chlorine, forming an ionic bond.

mcqs.question: The shape of a methane molecule is:
mcqs.explanation: Methane has four bonding pairs around carbon, giving a tetrahedral shape.

mcqs.question: One mole of any substance contains:
mcqs.explanation: Avogadro's number is 6.02 × 10^23 particles per mole.

mcqs.question: The molar mass of oxygen gas (O2) is:
mcqs.explanation: Oxygen has atomic mass 16, so O2 has a molar mass of 32 g/mol.

mcqs.question: The reagent that is completely consumed first in a reaction is the:
mcqs.explanation: The limiting reagent determines the maximum amount of product formed.

mcqs.question: According to kinetic molecular theory, gas pressure results from:
options: Random molecular collisions with container walls
mcqs.explanation: Gas pressure is produced when particles collide with the walls of the container.

mcqs.question: Which state of matter has definite volume but no definite shape?
mcqs.explanation: Liquids maintain volume but take the shape of their container.

mcqs.question: Boiling occurs when:
mcqs.explanation: A liquid boils when its vapor pressure becomes equal to the surrounding pressure.

mcqs.question: The functional group of alcohols is:
mcqs.explanation: Alcohols contain the hydroxyl functional group (-OH).

mcqs.question: Methane belongs to which homologous series?
mcqs.explanation: Methane is the simplest alkane.

mcqs.question: Unsaturated hydrocarbons contain at least one:
mcqs.explanation: Unsaturated compounds contain carbon-carbon double or triple bonds.

mcqs.question: The slope of a velocity-time graph represents:
mcqs.explanation: The gradient of a velocity-time graph gives acceleration.

mcqs.question: An object moving with constant velocity has:
mcqs.explanation: Constant velocity means no change in velocity, so acceleration is zero.

mcqs.question: Newton's second law can be written as:
mcqs.explanation: Newton's second law states that force equals mass times acceleration.

mcqs.question: The tendency of a body to resist change in its state of motion is called:
mcqs.explanation: Inertia is the resistance of an object to change in motion.

mcqs.question: Work done is maximum when the angle between force and displacement is:
mcqs.explanation: Work equals Fs cosθ, which is maximum at θ = 0°.

mcqs.question: The SI unit of power is:
mcqs.explanation: Power is measured in watts (J/s).

mcqs.question: Frequency is measured in:
mcqs.explanation: Hertz is the SI unit of frequency.

mcqs.question: In a longitudinal wave, particles vibrate:
mcqs.explanation: Longitudinal waves involve particle vibrations parallel to the direction of propagation.

mcqs.question: The SI unit of electric charge is:
mcqs.explanation: Electric charge is measured in coulombs.

mcqs.question: Like charges:
mcqs.explanation: Charges of the same type repel according to electrostatic principles.

mcqs.question: Choose the synonym of 'abundant'.
mcqs.explanation: Abundant means existing in large quantities; plentiful is the closest synonym.

mcqs.question: Choose the antonym of 'hostile'.
mcqs.explanation: Friendly is opposite in meaning to hostile.

mcqs.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: He go to college every day.
He goes to college every day.
He going to college every day.
He gone to college every day.
mcqs.explanation: With third person singular in present simple, the verb takes 's'.

mcqs.question: Select the correct article: She bought ___ umbrella.
mcqs.explanation: Umbrella begins with a vowel sound, so 'an' is correct.

mcqs.question: Identify the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.
options: The list of items are on the table.
The list of items is on the table.
The list of items were on the table.
The list of items have on the table.
mcqs.explanation: The subject is 'list', which is singular, so it takes 'is'.

mcqs.question: Choose the correct form: If I ___ enough money, I would buy a laptop.
mcqs.explanation: A second conditional sentence takes past simple in the if-clause.

mcqs.question: In reading comprehension, the main idea of a passage is the:
mcqs.explanation: The main idea is the author's central point or message.

mcqs.question: An inference is:
mcqs.explanation: An inference is a logical conclusion based on evidence in the text.

mcqs.question: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
mcqs.explanation: A doctor works in a hospital just as a teacher works in a classroom.

mcqs.question: Choose the best word to complete the meaning: Her explanation was so ___ that everyone understood it easily.
mcqs.explanation: Lucid means clear and easy to understand.
